fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kansas Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Kansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity kansas, how to transfer audiologist license to kansas, audiologist compact kansas, kansas audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Kansas Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS)
board.mailing_address: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services, Behavioral Health Services, 800 SW Jackson St., Suite 500, Topeka, KS 66612

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Kansas.

steps.title: Create an Account on the KDADS eLicense Portal
steps.description: Navigate to the KDADS eLicense Portal (kdads.my.site.com/portal/s/) and click 'New Account' to register your user profile. You will need to provide your email address and create a password.

steps.title: Initiate the Online Audiologist License Application
steps.description: Log into your newly created KDADS eLicense Portal account. From your dashboard, select the option to 'Apply for a License' and choose the Audiologist license application.

steps.title: Complete Application Sections and Upload Documents
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application, including personal information, educational history, and supervised clinical practicum details. Upload any necessary supporting documents directly through the KDADS eLicense Portal as requested.

steps.title: Arrange for Official Transcripts and Praxis Scores
steps.description: Contact your university to have official academic transcripts sent directly to the Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS). Also, ensure your Praxis Examination in Audiology scores are sent directly from ETS to KDADS.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee and Submit Your Application
steps.description: Once all sections are complete and documents are uploaded or arranged to be sent, pay the non-refundable $100 application fee through the KDADS eLicense Portal using a credit/debit card. Review your application thoroughly before final submission.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status and Await Board Review
steps.description: After submission, you can track the status of your application within your KDADS eLicense Portal account. The board typically processes complete applications within 4 to 6 weeks once all required documents are received.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Kansas audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Kansas have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Kansas participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Kansas without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Kansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Kansas
hero.dek: Audiologists in Kansas must apply for licensure through the KDADS eLicense Portal by creating an account and submitting an online application.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the KDADS eLicense Portal, click 'New Account,' and follow the prompts to create your user profile, which includes providing your email, creating a password, and setting security questions. After registration, you will need to verify your email address before logging in.
